title: arisarum vulgare subsp. subexertum
intro: Arisarum vulgare subsp. subexertum, also known as the yellow arum or Arum italicum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Arum family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's rhizome and roots contain medicinal compounds such as alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Arisarum vulgare subsp. subexertum include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being, and may be beneficial in reducing stress and improving sleep quality.
